senses.definition: someone who introduces news on a live TV or radio program by other broadcasters

examples.x: The anchor delivered the breaking news with a calm and authoritative demeanor.
She became a household name as the anchor of the primetime news program.
The network hired a seasoned journalist to serve as the anchor for its flagship morning show.
The anchor kept viewers informed during the storm with updates from the field reporters.
After decades in the industry, he retired as one of the most respected anchors in broadcast journalism.

senses.definition: a woman on TV or radio who tells people about the weather and describes it

examples.x: The weathergirl predicted sunny skies for the weekend getaway.
The weathergirl provides daily forecasts on the morning news program.
Viewers rely on the weathergirl's accurate predictions for planning outdoor activities.
The weathergirl uses state-of-the-art technology to track storms and predict weather patterns.
As a seasoned meteorologist, the weathergirl delivers weather updates with confidence and expertise.

senses.definition: someone who writes a piece to be published in a newspaper or magazine

examples.x: The magazine has a diverse group of contributors who write articles on a wide range of topics.
As a regular contributor to the local newspaper, she enjoys sharing her insights on community events.
The online publication accepts submissions from freelance contributors around the world.
The editor-in-chief praised the contributions of the staff writers and external contributors.
The magazine features a column written by a celebrity contributor each month.

senses.definition: someone employed by a TV or radio station or a newspaper to report news from a particular country or on a particular matter

examples.x: The network's Middle East correspondent provided updates on the conflict in the region.
As the White House correspondent, she covers the latest developments from the nation's capital.
The newspaper assigned a technology correspondent to report on advancements in the tech industry.
The radio station's sports correspondent delivers live commentary from major sporting events.

senses.definition: someone who sits around and watches TV a lot

examples.x: Don't be such a couch potato; get up and go for a walk!
His unhealthy lifestyle of being a couch potato is affecting his overall well-being.
If he starts going for regular walks, he can avoid becoming a couch potato.
Instead of going outside and exercising, he chooses to be a couch potato all day.
His lack of physical activity and constant TV watching have turned him into a couch potato.

senses.definition: freelance photographers who aggressively pursue and take pictures of celebrities, often in invasive or intrusive ways

examples.x: The paparazzi gathered outside the celebrity's home, hoping to catch a glimpse of the famous actor.
The paparazzi followed the couple everywhere they went, trying to capture candid shots for tabloid magazines.
Despite attempts to avoid attention, the singer was constantly hounded by the paparazzi.
The paparazzi's aggressive pursuit of celebrities has raised concerns about privacy and harassment.
The actress hired security to shield her from the paparazzi while attending the movie premiere.

senses.definition: someone who pays, at given intervals, to receive a publication or service

examples.x: The newspaper gained thousands of new subscribers after launching its digital edition.
As a subscriber to the magazine, she eagerly awaited each monthly issue.
The streaming service offers exclusive content to its subscribers.
The email newsletter has over a million subscribers worldwide.
New subscribers receive a welcome gift upon signing up for the service.

senses.definition: a device that is used to send and receive signals

examples.x: The satellite dish functions as an antenna to receive signals from space.
The car's antenna helps improve radio reception while driving in remote areas.
The scientist adjusted the antenna to optimize the reception of data from the distant probe.
The cellphone tower has multiple antennas to transmit and receive signals from mobile devices.

senses.definition: the specific number of waves that pass a point every second

examples.x: In physics, frequency is measured in hertz, which represents the number of waves passing a point per second.
The frequency of the sound waves determines the pitch of the sound.
The radio station broadcasts at a frequency of 101.5 megahertz.
Higher frequencies of light, such as ultraviolet and X-rays, have shorter wavelengths.

senses.definition: the distance between a point on a wave of energy and a similar point on the next wave

examples.x: In physics, wavelength is the distance between two consecutive peaks (or troughs) of a wave.
The color of light is determined by its wavelength, with shorter wavelengths appearing blue and longer wavelengths appearing red.
The wavelength of a radio wave determines its frequency and how it interacts with antennas.
Oceanographers study the wavelength of ocean waves to understand their behavior and predict coastal erosion.
The wavelength of sound waves affects the pitch of the sound, with shorter wavelengths producing higher pitches.

senses.definition: to remove parts of something such as a book, movie, etc. and prevent the public from accessing them for political, moral, or religious purposes

examples.x: The government decided to censor the film due to its sensitive content.
In some countries, authorities actively censor online content to control information flow.
The school library had to censor books with explicit content to ensure they were suitable for students.
During wartime, newspapers were often censored to prevent the release of sensitive information.

senses.definition: to pick up broadcast signals

examples.x: The television antenna receives digital signals from local broadcast stations, providing access to various channels.
Our satellite dish receives signals from satellites orbiting the Earth, allowing us to watch international news programs.
The radio receiver in the car receives FM and AM signals, enabling us to listen to music and news while driving.
The shortwave radio receiver can receive signals from distant countries, allowing listeners to tune in to international broadcasts.
The GPS receiver in the handheld device receives signals from satellites to determine its precise location.

senses.definition: to broadcast or show something on TV

examples.x: The network will televise the live coverage of the championship game.
The local station will televise the concert from the town square.
They plan to televise the debate between the political candidates.
The news program will televise the event as it unfolds.
The network will televise the special documentary on endangered species.

senses.definition: to watch a TV program or listen to a radio show

examples.x: Don't forget to tune in to the morning news for the latest updates.
Millions of viewers are expected to tune in to the season premiere of the hit TV show.
Listeners can tune in to the radio station for live coverage of the sporting event.
People from around the world can tune in online to watch the live stream of the concert.

senses.definition: a brief news program that is broadcast on the radio or television

examples.x: The radio station interrupted its regular programming to deliver a breaking news bulletin.
The school sends out a weekly bulletin to keep parents informed about upcoming events.
The police issued a bulletin describing the suspect they were searching for.
The hospital posted a bulletin in the lobby with information about visiting hours.
The company's CEO addressed employees in a bulletin regarding the upcoming changes to the organization.

senses.definition: a spoken description of an event while it is taking place, particularly on TV or radio

examples.x: The sports commentator provided live commentary during the soccer match.
Listeners tuned in to the radio station for the expert commentary on the political debate.
The television broadcast featured insightful commentary from political analysts.
The live stream of the awards ceremony included colorful commentary from the hosts.
The nature documentary was enhanced by the engaging commentary of the narrator.

senses.definition: an item of news that is given the most prominence in a news broadcast, magazine, or newspaper

examples.x: The scandal involving the politician was the lead story on every major news outlet.
The editor decided to make the environmental crisis the lead story in tomorrow's newspaper.
The network dedicated the first ten minutes of the broadcast to the lead story of the day.
The lead story on the website garnered the most views and comments from readers.
The magazine's lead story on health and wellness sparked a national conversation.

senses.definition: a place in radio or television stations or a newspaper office where news is reviewed and put together to be broadcast or published

examples.x: The journalists in the newsroom work tirelessly to gather and verify information for the evening broadcast.
The editor called a meeting in the newsroom to discuss the coverage of the upcoming election.
The newsroom buzzed with activity as reporters rushed to meet deadlines for the breaking story.
The intern was excited to land a position in the bustling newsroom of a major newspaper.
The newsroom was equipped with state-of-the-art technology to facilitate the production of high-quality content.

senses.definition: the time at which the largest number of people are watching TV or listening to the radio

examples.x: Advertisers pay top dollar for commercials aired during prime time television slots.
The new sitcom premieres tonight during prime time, attracting viewers of all ages.
The network's prime time lineup includes popular dramas and reality shows.
She enjoys watching her favorite game show every evening during prime time.
The news anchor delivers the evening broadcast during prime time, reaching millions of viewers.

senses.definition: the number of copies of a newspaper or magazine sold at regular intervals

examples.x: The newspaper's circulation figures reached an all-time high after the launch of its digital subscription service.
Despite the decline in print media, the magazine saw an increase in circulation thanks to its engaging content and multimedia platforms.
The publisher announced a decrease in circulation numbers, prompting a reassessment of distribution strategies.
Advertisers are particularly interested in publications with high circulation numbers as they offer greater exposure to potential customers.
The editor attributed the success of the magazine to its loyal readership, which has contributed to steady circulation figures over the years.

senses.definition: a story or article cut from a newspaper or magazine to be kept

examples.x: My grandmother keeps a box of cherished clippings that she has collected over the years, filled with articles of historical significance.
The journalist's office was cluttered with stacks of clippings, each representing a story they had written or found noteworthy.
She carefully added the latest clipping to her scrapbook, documenting memorable moments and achievements.
As a journalist, I often save clippings of my published articles as a record of my work and accomplishments.

senses.definition: the act of collecting news item for broadcast or publication

examples.x: Effective newsgathering requires journalists to adhere to ethical guidelines and verify information to maintain credibility with their audience.

senses.definition: a small and simple form of newspaper with only few pages

examples.x: The local community center distributes a weekly newssheet to keep residents informed about upcoming events and news.
The company sends out a daily newssheet to employees, highlighting important updates and announcements.
I subscribed to a newssheet from my favorite online magazine to receive curated news and articles in my inbox.
The organization publishes a monthly newssheet featuring stories about their humanitarian efforts around the world.

senses.definition: one of a series of publications produced regularly

examples.x: The latest issue of the magazine features in-depth interviews with industry leaders and analysis of current trends.
I eagerly anticipate each new issue of the comic book series, eager to follow the ongoing storylines of my favorite characters.
The editorial team worked tirelessly to meet the deadline for the quarterly issue of the scholarly journal.
Subscribers can access the digital archive of past issues, allowing them to revisit articles from previous editions.
The newspaper's investigative report in last week's issue sparked a public debate on government transparency.

senses.definition: the act or profession of reporting news articles in newspapers or magazines mainly through photographs

examples.x: The power of photojournalism lies in its ability to capture raw emotions and tell compelling stories in a single image.
Many iconic moments in history have been immortalized through the lens of photojournalism, shaping our collective memory of significant events.
The Pulitzer Prize for Breaking News Photography recognizes outstanding achievements in the field of photojournalism.
With the rise of social media, amateur photojournalism has become more prevalent, allowing ordinary individuals to document and share news events in real-time.

senses.definition: an occasion arranged by a politician or other famous people to be photographed while doing something that they think will popularize them

examples.x: The politician staged a photo op at the local hospital to highlight their commitment to healthcare reform.
The celebrity's publicist arranged a photo op at a charity event to generate positive publicity.
The company organized a photo op with employees volunteering at a community cleanup project to promote corporate social responsibility.
The mayor's office scheduled a photo op with schoolchildren to raise awareness for an upcoming education initiative.
The museum curator arranged a photo op with a famous artist to promote an upcoming exhibition.

senses.definition: a publication, especially about a technical subject, that is produced regularly

examples.x: My local library carries a wide selection of periodicals, including magazines, journals, and newspapers.
I enjoy reading the latest issue of my favorite periodical over breakfast every morning.
As a subscriber to several periodicals, I look forward to receiving each new issue in the mail.

senses.definition: a publication produced four times a year

examples.x: I subscribed to a literary quarterly that features short stories, poetry, and essays from emerging writers.
The nonprofit organization sends out a quarterly to its donors, highlighting the impact of their contributions.
The university library receives a range of academic quarterlies, offering in-depth research articles across various disciplines.

senses.definition: a newspaper with smaller pages and many pictures, covering stories about famous people and not much serious news

examples.x: The tabloid sensationalized the celebrity scandal with exaggerated headlines and paparazzi photos.
Despite its reputation for gossip, some tabloids also cover serious news stories alongside celebrity gossip.
The supermarket checkout line is lined with tabloids featuring the latest celebrity gossip and scandalous headlines.
The tabloid's front page featured a shocking exposé about a politician's personal life.
Tabloids often rely on anonymous sources and speculative reporting to attract readers with sensational stories.

senses.definition: the number of people who read a particular magazine, newspaper, or book on a regular basis

examples.x: The magazine's readership has grown steadily over the past year, thanks to engaging content and targeted marketing efforts.
The newspaper conducted a survey to better understand its readership demographics and preferences.
The author's latest book has attracted a diverse readership, spanning multiple age groups and interests.
The online publication tracks readership metrics to assess the popularity of articles and topics.
The editors strive to cater to their readership's interests by featuring a variety of content in each issue.

senses.definition: the coverage of news by ordinary people, which is then shared on the Internet

examples.x: With the rise of social media, citizen journalism has become a powerful tool for ordinary people to report on breaking news events.
Citizen journalism played a crucial role in documenting and sharing real-time updates during the protests.
Many news organizations now include citizen journalism in their coverage, often sourcing photos and videos from on-the-ground witnesses.
The impact of citizen journalism is evident in how quickly information can spread from local incidents to global awareness.
While citizen journalism offers fresh perspectives, it also raises concerns about the accuracy and verification of information shared by non-professional reporters.

senses.definition: (of a person) not known by name

examples.x: The whistleblower chose to remain anonymous to protect their identity and avoid potential retaliation.
An anonymous donor contributed a large sum of money to the charity, helping to fund its new outreach program.
The artist behind the popular street murals has remained anonymous, adding an element of mystery to their work.
The journalist received an anonymous tip that led to the uncovering of a major corruption scandal.

senses.definition: drawing a lot of public attention or interest

examples.x: The high-profile trial captured media attention nationwide.
The celebrity's high-profile relationship garnered constant media scrutiny and public interest.
The high-profile social media influencer's endorsement led to a surge in product sales.
The company's high-profile product launch attracted attention from industry experts and consumers alike.
The scandal involving a high-profile public figure dominated headlines for weeks, sparking intense public interest and debate.

senses.definition: existing or occurring across a country

examples.x: The company's nationwide advertising campaign reached consumers in every corner of the country.
The nationwide vaccination campaign aimed to immunize the entire population against the virus.
The government implemented a nationwide vaccination program to combat the spread of the virus.
The telecommunications company offers nationwide coverage, ensuring connectivity for users wherever they travel within the country.
The nationwide ban on smoking in public places improved air quality and public health.

senses.definition: one of the main methods of radio broadcasting with a high sound quality

examples.x: Frequency modulation is commonly used in FM radio broadcasting to transmit high-fidelity sound over long distances.
Engineers prefer frequency modulation over amplitude modulation for its resistance to signal interference and noise.
The scientist explained how frequency modulation alters the frequency of the carrier wave to encode information.
Many modern communication systems, including two-way radios and television broadcasts, rely on frequency modulation for clear signal transmission.
Understanding frequency modulation is essential for anyone studying electronics and communication engineering.

senses.definition: a piece of paper on which a vote is written

examples.x: Each voter received a ballot to mark their choices in the local election.
The ballot included options for mayor, city council members, and several local referendums.
After casting her ballot, she felt proud to have participated in the democratic process.
The election officials carefully counted each ballot to ensure an accurate result.
The ballot was designed to be simple and clear to help voters make informed decisions.

senses.definition: a service delivering written news and information through television, currently replaced by other information services provided on a television network

examples.x: The decline of teletext began with the rise of the internet and digital broadcasting technologies.
Some countries still use teletext for public service announcements and emergency alerts on television channels.
